4/4 unit tests pass — proves the HybridRecall.search() graceful-degradation contract at the data-flow level. (i) b1_qdrant_unavailable_returns_bm25_hits_with_degraded_flag: bogus Qdrant URL (127.0.0.1:1) via new QdrantStore::open_lazy + OkEmbedder + SQLite FTS5 corpus -> envelope.degraded.qdrant_unavailable=true, hits non-empty, includes the "ollama" BM25 doc. (ii) b2_ollama_unavailable_returns_bm25_hits_with_degraded_flag: FlakyEmbedder always-Err + SQLite -> envelope.degraded.ollama_unavailable=true, hits include the "rust" BM25 doc; Qdrant never reached so qdrant_unavailable stays false. (iii) both_vector_lanes_dead_and_no_sqlite_returns_err_actionable: FlakyEmbedder + lazy Qdrant + no SQLite -> Err mentions "all retrieval lanes unavailable" + names which deps. (iv) bm25_only_envelope_has_qdrant_flag_set_but_returns_hits: per-call envelope semantics locked. Refactor: introduced Embedder trait (Rust 2024 native async-fn-in-traits) so HybridRecall<E: Embedder = OllamaClient> is generic for test injection; QdrantStore::open_lazy() builds gRPC client without ensure_collection; HybridRecall::search() returns RecallEnvelope { hits, degraded } and emits tracing::warn! lines (target="obelion.recall") on each lane failure + tracing::error! when both lanes die. MCP server now wires SQLite at startup so production has BM25 lane available for graceful degradation (resolve_sqlite_path: OBELION_DATA_DIR env or <config_dir>/obelion/obelion.db default). MCP recall tool description updated to advertise the envelope shape. Live-shell B1/B2 harness (kill the qdrant container mid-test, observe WARN line in stderr) deferred to a follow-up turn — needs the harness scaffolding around `obelion-mcp` stdio + ensure_daemons.ps1 inversion (stop, not start).

3/3 tests pass. (i) concurrent_multi_writer_no_panics_correct_total: 4 tokio spawn_blocking writers each running 250 insert_observation calls against the SAME SQLite file via independent SqliteStore handles (modeling separate processes); final count_observations()=1000 exact; verify_full_chain() passes — the hash chain is unbroken across all 1000 concurrent inserts. (ii) read_during_write_no_lock_panic: writer + reader tokio tasks hammer the same file simultaneously (writer doing inserts, reader doing bm25_search); WAL mode allows the concurrent reader without SQLITE_BUSY panic. (iii) wal_mode_is_actually_set_on_file_backed_store: PRAGMA journal_mode reports "wal" — locks the production posture contract. Discovered + fixed a real concurrency bug during the test: insert_observation was reading last_observation_hash() OUTSIDE the transaction, so two writers saw the same head and chain-forked (test caught it: "chain broken at index 56"). Fix: BEGIN IMMEDIATE wraps both the head-read and the row INSERT in one atomic transaction. SqliteStore::init_schema now sets PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL + synchronous=NORMAL + busy_timeout=5s on every open. Documented in docs/concurrency.md.

`obelion doctor` ran live against current host and reported all 4 checks PASS in JSON: ollama_api_version (GET http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/version -> 200 OK), qdrant_http_readyz (GET http://127.0.0.1:6333/readyz -> 200 OK), sqlite_store (path=C:\Users\anto\AppData\Roaming\obelion\obelion.db journal_mode=wal observations=0 — proves WAL + writable + schema-init), data_dir_writable (probe-write to AppData/Roaming/obelion). New Doctor subcommand on CLI: emits DoctorReport { version, checks: [{name, verdict: PASS|FAIL|WARN, detail}], all_pass: bool } as JSON; exits non-zero if any FAIL. Each FAIL detail includes remediation hint (`ollama serve` / `docker run qdrant/qdrant:v1.15.4`). Pre-creates data dir on probe (mkdir -p semantics) so first-run doctor never falsely fails on missing dir. Halves support cost per addendum's E4 rationale.

New crate-level module crates/core/src/timeout.rs (74 lines): pub const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=4000, pub fn resolve_timeout() reads OBELION_TIMEOUT_MS with 100ms floor, pub fn parse_timeout(Option<&str>) is the pure-function impl (no env mutation in tests — avoids the test-parallel-env-race that the first cut hit), pub async fn with_timeout<F,T>(d, fut) wraps any Result-future with a hard cap; on Err returns "timeout after Xs" so the existing degraded-path match handles it like any other dep failure. recall.rs search() now wraps embed_query + qdrant.search in with_timeout(resolve_timeout(), ...) per call. Pattern composes cleanly with the B1+B2 envelope flags from commit 2a9c64d — timeouts are just another flavor of lane outage.
